mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, equity discount rates ease faster than earnings expectations deteriorate, so S&P 500 prices rise over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary labor damage that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are negative in most periods with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend forces higher real-rate expectations and tighter policy pricing, which compresses equity multiples and pushes S&P 500 lower over the next month. The effect weakens when inflation is decelerating even if the level remains high.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month over month for two consecutive releases
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise trigger events are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional Rank IC is not negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and hurts equities over the next quarter. The decline should be strongest when payroll growth is also soft and joblessness is moving upward simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-weakening episodes do not produce negative 60d SPX returns on average, or if positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggers despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in RSAFS indicates resilient consumer spending and revenue growth for cyclicals and large-cap firms, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when sales strength is offset by an aggressive rate-hike backdrop.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend retail sales do not correspond to positive 20d SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year information coefficient is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling GDPC1 and weak RSAFS identifies demand contraction, which lowers earnings forecasts and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when both series deteriorate together rather than separately.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarter and RSAFS is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if combined GDP-sales weakness fails to predict negative 60d returns, or if the joint signal is no better than either proxy alone across rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and earnings momentum, which supports risk assets over the next week through stronger growth expectations. The signal breaks down when industrial output rises only because of inventory restocking without demand follow-through.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after INDPRO upticks, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises while INDPRO is also improving, the economy is in a synchronized real-activity expansion that supports cyclical earnings and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if housing strength occurs during tightening that suppresses demand elsewhere.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month over month and INDPRO is above its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-industrial trigger does not lead to positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS growth stays positive while UNRATE remains stable or falls, labor income is still expanding and consumption can support equity prices over the next quarter. The signal fails when payroll gains are concentrated in low-quality jobs and unemployment begins rising anyway.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over the latest month and UNRATE is flat or lower than 3 months ago
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains with stable unemployment do not produce positive 60d SPX returns on average, or if negative outcomes dominate despite the trigger being active.
